areas.name: Parks Operations and Athletic Field Services
areas.context: The division maintains 66 city parks covering more than 1,000 acres, including athletic fields, courts, playgrounds, shelters, and open space.
key_considerations: Playground safety inspection standards [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Weather hold and closure protocols
ADA pathway maintenance
Public notification of closures
Contractor oversight
areas.format_note: Weather and emergency closure decisions are Format Type 2.

areas.name: Facilities Maintenance Services
areas.context: The division maintains all city facilities — community centers, recreation centers, parks buildings, and administrative facilities. A new Enterprise Asset Management system is being implemented.
key_considerations: City safety and risk management requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
OSHA general industry standards for tradesworkers [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Contractor insurance and bonding [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — confirm with City Procurement]
Chemical storage
Key and access control coordination with facility supervisors

areas.name: Urban Forestry Maintenance
areas.context: Manages all city street trees and park trees. Rockville maintains Tree City USA status. The City maintains thousands of street trees across all neighborhoods.
common_processes: Annual pruning schedule development and execution
Hazardous tree assessment and removal authorization
key_considerations: ISA certified arborist practices [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
ANSI A300 pruning standards [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Maryland Forest Service requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Tree protection during construction
Public notification for street tree removal
areas.format_note: Emergency storm response is Format Type 2 (Decision-Support).

areas.name: Horticultural Services
areas.context: Staff plan and maintain seasonal flower and bulb displays at locations throughout the city. Horticultural staff also coordinate with Croydon Creek on the Native Plant Sale.
key_considerations: Maryland pesticide applicator license requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — contact Maryland Department of Agriculture]
Chemical storage and disposal [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Integrated pest management (IPM) protocols

areas.name: Trails and Open Space Services
key_roles: Trails and Open Spaces Crew Supervisor
areas.context: Manages city trails, open spaces, and rights-of-way. Supports Weed Warrior and Adopt a Stream volunteer events. Invasive species management is an ongoing operational priority.
common_processes: Invasive species identification, treatment, and monitoring
key_considerations: Invasive species treatment chemical requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Volunteer liability and waiver requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — confirm with City Risk Management]
ADA trail surface standards [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Stormwater permit requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — coordinate with DPW Environmental Management Division]

areas.name: Fleet Services and Vehicle Management
cost_centers: Part of Senior Citizen Services (fleet primarily serves senior transportation); coordination with City Fleet Services for maintenance [VERIFY scope with department]
areas.context: CDL-licensed drivers operate City-owned vehicles for senior transportation. The fleet supports aging-in-place transportation goals including early morning, evening, and weekend routes.
common_processes: Passenger boarding and ADA lift operation
CDL license and medical certificate tracking
key_considerations: CDL requirements for bus drivers [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
ADA paratransit accessibility requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
City vehicle use policy [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — confirm applicable City AR]
Insurance and liability for passenger transport
Senior-specific boarding assistance protocols
areas.format_note: Vehicle pre-trip and post-trip inspections are Format Type 5 (Job Aid/Checklist).
